attacking_style: Thrives in attritional, chaotic sprint finishes where the peloton has been fractured by challenging conditions. Excels in uphill sprints after long, hard stages that thin out pure sprinters.
team_tactics: Limited tactical data available, but benefits from race situations where natural attrition replaces organized lead-outs, allowing him to position independently in reduced groups.
terrain_preference: Uphill sprint finishes after brutally long stages with headwinds or adverse weather. Exposed terrain that fragments the field suits his resilience better than flat, organized bunch sprints.
vulnerability: Injury prone, particularly crashes affecting lower body. Knee injuries specifically mentioned as compromising sprint form, suggesting physical durability concerns in Grand Tour stage sequences.
key_patterns: Wins from fractured pelotons after extreme distance or weather creates natural selection (Vuelta 143.5mi stage).
Uphill sprint capability separates him from pure flat sprinters in reduced groups.
Crash vulnerability raises questions about maintaining form through three-week stage sequences.
form_trajectory: negative: Most recent observation shows crash-related injury with doubts raised about sprint form for subsequent stages, indicating fragility in stage racing
